DATAVALUE: Avenida Santa Rosa 11315, La Pintana, Santiago, Chile

Located at the Senda Darwin Biological Station, in a old-growth rainforest. Locally named "pomponal"  from the common name of Sphagnum, is an anthropogenic peatland, formed after the forest is cut or a forest fire. The station is 15 km east of Ancud, in the Chiloé Island, close to the Huicha river.
Avenida Santa Rosa 11315, La Pintana, Santiago, Chile
National Commission for Scientific & Technological Research of Chile (grants FONDEQUIP AIC-37, FONDECYT 1130935, FONDECYT 1171239)
